zotero.abstractNote: Actor-critic methods are a popular class of reinforcement learning algorithms that combine the benefits of policy-based and value-based methods. However, they often suffer from high variance and instability, especially when using function approximation. In this paper, we propose a new class of actor-critic methods that use temporal-difference (TD) regularization to improve the stability and performance of the learning process. We show that our proposed methods can be interpreted as a form of natural policy gradient, and we provide theoretical guarantees for their convergence. We also demonstrate the effectiveness of our methods on a variety of reinforcement learning benchmarks, showing that they outperform existing actor-critic methods in terms of both sample efficiency and final performance.

extra.Editors: Karsten Borgwardt, Po-Ling Loh, Evimaria Terzi, and Antti Ukkonen
